response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile full-stack engineer with 13+ years across web, mobile, and embedded work, but the role centers on professional C++/STL development for industrial automation, which is largely absent here. C++ does not appear in the skills list; the only C/C++ exposure is a ~6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis. There is no evidence of message-broker architectures, MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA, multi-threaded C++ systems, or IEC 62443 / SDL secure-engineering experience. The strong TypeScript/Node/DLang background does not transfer cleanly to the core technical and security requirements of this position.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering experience (13+ years) with low-latency, high-throughput systems
Strong software-craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing, mentoring/workshops
Some C/C++ exposure (National Instruments IDE components, audio thesis) and event-driven platform work
concerns: C++/STL is not a demonstrated core skill; only ~6 months professional C/C++ a decade ago
No industrial-automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA, or message-broker experience
Career is web/full-stack (JS/TS/Node/DLang)-centric, misaligned with the role's systems/security focus

key_factors.factor: Missing industrial cybersecurity / IEC 62443 / SDL background
Strong general engineering and high-performance systems experience
